---
title: "Completion Time : Exploratory Data Analysis"
author: "CongLiu"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
    df_print: paged
  word_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 5,
  fig.align = "center",
  dpi = 300
)

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
library(tidyverse)
library(ggdist)      # raincloud support
library(patchwork)
library(rstatix)
})
```

## 1. Data import and preprocessing
```{r data}
# ---- Data location ----
# The raw dataset is stored outside this analysis folder (e.g., in a sibling directory).
# If your folder layout differs, update `candidate_dirs` below.
data_file <- "Experimental_Raw_Data.csv"
candidate_dirs <- c(
  file.path("..", "data"),
  file.path("..", "datasets"),
  ".."
)
candidate_paths <- file.path(candidate_dirs, data_file)
data_path <- candidate_paths[file.exists(candidate_paths)][1]

if (is.na(data_path)) {
  stop("Data file not found. Checked:
  - ", paste(candidate_paths, collapse = "
  - "))
}

dat <- readr::read_csv(data_path, show_col_types = FALSE) %>% 
  mutate(
    group       = factor(group, levels = c("3D-only","2D+3D")),
    complexity  = factor(complexity, levels = c("simple","complex")),
    participant = factor(participant),
    log_ct      = log(completion_time)
  )
glimpse(dat)
```

## 2. Descriptive statistics and normality checks
```{r desc}
dat %>% 
  summarise(
    mean_sec = mean(completion_time),
    sd_sec   = sd(completion_time),
    shapiro_W = shapiro_test(completion_time)$statistic,
    shapiro_p = shapiro_test(completion_time)$p.value
  )

dat %>% 
  summarise(
    mean_log = mean(log_ct),
    sd_log   = sd(log_ct),
    shapiro_W = shapiro_test(log_ct)$statistic,
    shapiro_p = shapiro_test(log_ct)$p.value
  )
```

## 3. Distributional assessment: raw vs. log-transformed
```{r histo}
p1 <- ggplot(dat, aes(completion_time)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Raw seconds")

p2 <- ggplot(dat, aes(log_ct)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Log-transformed")

p1 + p2
```

## 4. Raincloud plots (raw and log scales)
```{r raincloud}
p_raw <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = interaction(group, complexity, sep = '\n'), 
                         y = completion_time)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = .5, width = .6, .width = 0, point_colour = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = .4) +
  geom_jitter(width = .05, height = 0, size = 1.2, alpha = .5) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Completion time (s)") +
  theme_minimal()

p_log <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = interaction(group, complexity, sep = '\n'), 
                         y = log_ct)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = .5, width = .6, .width = 0, point_colour = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = .4) +
  geom_jitter(width = .05, height = 0, size = 1.2, alpha = .5) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Log completion time") +
  theme_minimal()

p_raw / p_log
```

---
**Note.** We first assess right-skewness and normality on the raw scale, then illustrate how the log transform improves symmetry to motivate the subsequent LMM analyses.
